children.name: ValidateCommand
children.purpose: Validate that the command is one of the four allowed values and that required fields in message_data are present based on command type.
children.behavior: Check command against allowed set; for each command, verify required keys in message_data (e.g., channel_id for send/history/join, channel_name for create_channel, content for send). Return error if invalid.
children.signature: def ValidateCommand(command: str, message_data: dict) -> dict

constraints.description: Must not modify any global state
Must return error dict for invalid commands

acceptance_criteria.description: Returns success=True for valid command and required fields
Returns success=False with message for invalid command
Returns success=False with message for missing required fields

children.stop_reason: Pure validation logic, no further decomposition needed

children.name: SendMessage
children.purpose: Handle the 'send' command: verify user membership, store message, update user's last_seen, notify other channel members.
children.behavior: Check if user_id is in channel's members list; if not, return error. Generate new message_id, store message in messages store with timestamp. Update user's last_seen in users store. Notify other channel members (simulated as adding to a notification list or logging). Return success with message data.
children.signature: def SendMessage(message_data: dict, channels: dict, messages: dict, users: dict) -> dict

global_vars.description: Write new message to messages store
Read user data for membership check and last_seen update
Read channel members list for membership verification

data_operations.description: Read user data for last_seen update

constraints.description: Must verify user is a member of the channel before storing message
Must generate unique message_id for each message
Must include timestamp in stored message

acceptance_criteria.description: Returns success=True with message data when user is member
Returns success=False when user is not a member
Message is stored with correct channel_id, user_id, content, timestamp
User's last_seen is updated after successful send

children.stop_reason: Can be further decomposed into membership check, store, update, notify

children.name: GetHistory
children.purpose: Handle the 'history' command: retrieve last 100 messages from a channel sorted by timestamp descending.
children.behavior: Query messages store for all messages with given channel_id, sort by timestamp descending, limit to 100, return as list.
children.signature: def GetHistory(message_data: dict, messages: dict) -> dict

global_vars.description: Read messages from messages store filtered by channel_id

constraints.description: Must sort messages by timestamp descending

acceptance_criteria.description: Returns success=True with list of messages for valid channel_id
Returns empty list if channel has no messages
Messages are sorted by timestamp descending

children.stop_reason: Atomic read operation with sorting and limit

children.name: CreateChannel
children.purpose: Handle the 'create_channel' command: create new channel with unique name, set caller as creator and first member.
children.behavior: Check if channel_name already exists in channels store; if so, return error. Generate new channel_id, store channel with name, members list containing user_id, created_by set to user_id. Return success with channel data.
children.signature: def CreateChannel(message_data: dict, channels: dict) -> dict

global_vars.description: Read existing channels for uniqueness check, write new channel

constraints.description: Must ensure channel_name is unique before creating
Must set user_id as first member and creator

acceptance_criteria.description: Returns success=True with channel data when name is unique
Returns success=False when channel_name already exists
New channel has correct name, members list containing user_id, created_by set to user_id

children.stop_reason: Single atomic operation with uniqueness check

children.name: JoinChannel
children.purpose: Handle the 'join' command: add user to channel's member list if not already a member.
children.behavior: Check if user_id is already in channel's members list; if so, return error. Otherwise, add user_id to members list in channels store. Return success with updated member list.
children.signature: def JoinChannel(message_data: dict, channels: dict) -> dict

global_vars.description: Read channel members list, write updated members list

data_operations.description: Update channel members list with new user

constraints.description: Must verify user is not already a member before adding
Must update the members list atomically

acceptance_criteria.description: Returns success=True with updated members list when user not already member
Returns success=False when user is already a member
User_id is added to channel's members list on success

children.stop_reason: Single atomic operation with membership check

governance_notes: Parent global state conservation verified: messages (read_write) covered by SendMessage (write) and GetHistory (read); users (read) covered by SendMessage (read); channels (read_write) covered by SendMessage (read), CreateChannel (read_write), JoinChannel (read_write). All parent required operations are satisfied by child global_vars. No new variables introduced. ValidateCommand is a pure function with no global state access.
